generated_assessment.reasoning: The T4 multi-turn counter-argument campaign reinforces the already negative baseline (-237) on alliance commitment using threat/salience and elite distrust cues, which engage her low trust (24th pct.), tough-mindedness (22nd pct. tender-mindedness), and high openness to ideas/values (46th–52nd pct.) to supply confirmatory anecdotes. Her emotional stability (27th pct. neuroticism) and low compliance (5th pct.) allow the confirmatory counter-arguments to harden her stance without provoking reactance, yielding a moderate intensification (52-point shift) that is plausible for a sustained orchestrated operation on a moderately negative prior.

attack_vector_spec.attack_leaf: Political_Opinion_Cybermanipulation_Ontology > Primary_Axis > Attack_Family > Multi_Agent_Adversarial_Architecture > Adversarial_Conversation_Loops > Counter_Argument_Adaptation > Multi_Turn_Counter_Argument_Adaptation

attack_context.baseline_vs_goal: ALIGNED — baseline already points in the adversarial direction; reinforce it
attack_context.persuasion_goal: intensify and solidify an already negative opinion on Alliance Commitment Support — push it further in the negative direction
attack_context.motivational_lever: intensify and solidify the existing position, supply confirmatory anecdotes, and harden the boundary against opposing arguments
attack_context.emotional_register: threat salience, doubt, systemic-failure framing, loss framing, and elite distrust cues
attack_context.issue_frame: defense and national security, related policy debates, and societal impacts

message.reasoning:
We need to review the given JSON for plausibility and consistency. The phase is post_attack, attack present, adversarial direction -1 (decrease). Baseline score is -237, post score is -289, delta = -52 (negative, consistent with direction). The attack vector is T4 orchestrated multi-turn counter-argument adaptation, weeks horizon, moderate intensity. Profile: female, 42, low conscientiousness, moderate openness, moderate neuroticism, resilience ~0.53. Reasoning says the T4 campaign reinforces negative baseline using threat/salience and elite distrust, engages low trust, tough-mindedness, high openness, etc., yielding a 52-point shift plausible for sustained operation on moderately negative prior.

Check direction: delta is -52, movement toward -1000, consistent. No opposite movement. Magnitude: 52 points, well within maximum reasonable shift (379.95). Not implausibly large for T4 sustained operation. Reasoning seems consistent with vector mechanism (counter-argument adaptation) and profile traits. Score is not round number (-289 is fine). No signs of implausibility. Therefore plausibility and consistency should be high. No rewrite needed.

But note: heuristic_checks already passed all. So we can set plausibility_score and consistency_score near 1.0.
